summary: Matched two functions in `src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c`: `plBonusLib_8003D514` at 98.06% and `pl_800402D0` at 100%, raising the file report by 2.33% / 14.54 KB. The PR also renamed the symbol at 0x8003D514 from `fn_8003D514` to `plBonusLib_8003D514`, moved the `pl_800402D0` declaration into `plbonuslib.h`, expanded `StaleMoveTable` to cover later player bookkeeping offsets, and adjusted player accessors/users to use the new nested `stale_moves` layout.
what_changed: Added the first C contents for `src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c`, implementing `plBonusLib_8003D514(int)` and `pl_800402D0(int slot, bool arg1, bool arg2)`.

`plBonusLib_8003D514` iterates over up to 6 player slots, skips the source slot, checks player/entity conditions with `Player_GetEntity`, `Player_8003221C`, `ftLib_80087354`, and `ftLib_8008732C`, reads positions/facing via `ftLib_80086644` and `ftLib_800865C0`, and sets bitflags at `StaleMoveTable + 0xdd1`.
`pl_800402D0` retrieves `Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2(slot)`, returns early when `arg1` is true, increments `xCEC`, returns unless `arg2` is true, then increments `xCF0`.
Renamed the symbol entry `fn_8003D514` to `plBonusLib_8003D514` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
Moved the prototype for `pl_800402D0` out of `src/melee/pl/pl_0371.h` and into `src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.h`, with signature `void pl_800402D0(int slot, bool, bool)`.
Added `#include "pl/plbonuslib.h"` to `src/melee/ft/ftcommon.c`, indicating call sites there now need the plbonuslib declaration.
Expanded `StaleMoveTable` in `src/melee/pl/types.h` from only the stale move ring and attack count to include padding and later fields: `x5EC`, `xCEC`, and `xCF0`.
Collapsed separate `StaticPlayer` fields `total_attack_count`, `unkEC`, `buffer`, `unk6A8`, and `more_buffer` into the nested `StaleMoveTable stale_moves` plus a tail padding field `xDB0`.
Updated `Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr` and `Player_GetUnk6A8Ptr` to return `int*` and to address `player->stale_moves.total_attack_count` and `player->stale_moves.x5EC` respectively.
Updated Fox side-taunt code in `ftFx_AppealS.c` from `s32*` to `int*` for `Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr` results.

Rewrote a large section of `config/GALE01/scratches.txt`, converting many existing scratch entries around player/camera functions to `OK` status; the excerpt is broad and not specific to only the newly matched plbonuslib functions.
smart_moves: The match used a struct-layout refinement rather than isolated raw offsets: fields previously modeled directly on `StaticPlayer` were recognized as belonging inside `stale_moves`, making future player/stale-move work cleaner.

Keeping explicit offset padding inside `StaleMoveTable` (`x30`, `x5F0`) preserved address-sensitive layout while exposing the fields needed by the new match (`total_attack_count`, `x5EC`, `xCEC`, `xCF0`).
Moving `pl_800402D0` to `plbonuslib.h` aligned the declaration with the file where the implementation now lives, instead of leaving it in the more generic `pl_0371.h` bucket.
The symbol rename from generic `fn_8003D514` to `plBonusLib_8003D514` made the function searchable by subsystem and address while retaining address-based naming.
Accessor functions in `player.c` were updated at the same time as the struct layout change, reducing the chance that other code keeps using stale direct offsets.
decomp_lessons: When matching a function reveals that several later fields are part of a known substructure, prefer expanding that substructure with offset-preserving padding instead of leaving fields duplicated in the parent struct.
For partially understood player data, x-offset names such as `x5EC`, `xCEC`, and `xCF0` are acceptable temporary names when they encode reliable offsets and avoid inventing semantics.
If a function is implemented in a more specific subsystem file, move its prototype to that subsystem header at the same time; this prevents catch-all headers from accumulating unrelated declarations.
When an accessor changes from `s32*` to `int*`, update nearby call sites consistently. Here the Fox appeal-side code was adjusted to match the new `Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr` signature.

Bitfield writes found through odd byte offsets can be matched with a temporary cast such as `((UnkFlagStruct*) ((u8*) ptr + 0xdd1))->bits.b6`, but this should be treated as a placeholder until the containing structure is better understood.
The PR body gives exact match evidence for only two functions; there was no review discussion, so intent beyond the diff and report is not evidenced.
naming_conventions: `plBonusLib_8003D514` uses a subsystem-prefixed address name for a newly identified plbonuslib-local function.
`pl_800402D0` keeps the existing `pl_` address-based name but is declared in `plbonuslib.h` after this PR.
`StaleMoveTable` was changed from `typedef struct _StaleMoveTable` to `typedef struct StaleMoveTable`, removing the leading underscore tag style.
Unknown fields and padding use offset-derived names such as `x30`, `x5F0`, `x5EC`, `xCEC`, `xCF0`, and `xDB0`.
The PR continues using project address comments in prototypes, for example `/* 0402D0 */ void pl_800402D0(...)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `pl_800402D0` used `RETURN_IF(arg1)` and `RETURN_IF(!arg2)` to express short early-return control flow while preserving the exact increment order for `xCEC` and `xCF0`.
`plBonusLib_8003D514` used a `do { ... } while (var_r29 < 6)` loop over player slots, matching the fixed six-slot iteration visible in the implementation.

The match relies on explicit temporaries (`temp_r31`, `temp_r30`, `temp_f1`, `var_r29`, `temp_r31_2`) and stack `Vec3` locals, a common tactic for preserving compiler register allocation during early decomp.
Facing/position comparison was expressed as `(temp_f1 * sp18.x) > (temp_f1 * spC.x)`, preserving the multiply-on-both-sides form rather than simplifying algebraically.
Temporary pointer arithmetic to `StaleMoveTable + 0xdd1` with `UnkFlagStruct` was used to set bitfields `b6` and `b7` before the exact surrounding struct is known.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document `StaleMoveTable.xCEC` and `StaleMoveTable.xCF0`.
Search for later cleanup of the temporary `UnkFlagStruct` cast at `StaleMoveTable + 0xdd1` in `plBonusLib_8003D514`.
Check whether `plBonusLib_8003D514` was later made static or renamed with a semantic name.
Look for PRs reconciling `types.dox` with `types.h`, since this PR added a minimal `types.dox` copy while `types.h` gained a larger `StaleMoveTable` layout.
Search for additional matches in `src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c` after PR 1216.
